text: United States Toxic Substances Control Act Section 6(h) (Restriction of certain substances) In February 2021 the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) published in section 6 (h) the restriction of 5 substances potentially present in Electronic Equipment. Two of these substances aren’t controlled by European legislation. https://www.epa.gov/assessing-and-managing-chemicals-under-tsca/persistent-bioaccumulative-and-toxic-pbt-chemicals-under Following substances are restricted: 2,4,6-Tris(tert-butyl)phenol (2,4,6-TTBP) CAS 732-26-3 Decabromodiphenyl ether (DecaBDE) CAS 1163-19-5 Phenol, isopropylated phosphate (3:1) (PIP (3:1)) CAS 68937-41-7 2,4,6-Tris(tert-butyl)phenol Hexachlorobutadiene (HCBD) CAS 87-68-3 Pentachlorothiophenol (PCTP) CAS 133-49-3 HARTING products don’t contain substances TTBP, DecaDBE and HCBD. The distribution of PIP (3:1) is allowed until September 2024. The distribution of PCTP is allowed until January 2022. Substance PCTP and PIP (3:1) also are not contained in HARTING products. Based on the supplier replies we don’t expect to have any of the above mentioned substances in HARTING articles. February 2025
